frameworks.name: Bayesian perceptual inference
frameworks.one_line: Biases arise when noisy sensory measurements combine with a prior over stimuli.

frameworks.name: central tendency bias
frameworks.one_line: Delayed estimates pull toward the set mean; extended from magnitude to color.

core_question: How do stimulus range and internal/external noise shape the central tendency bias in delayed color estimation?
key_assumptions: Observers combine a learned prior over the stimulus set with a noisy sensory likelihood
Increasing delay raises internal memory noise; adding chromatic noise raises external sensory noise
manipulations: Hue range of the stimulus set (different prior distributions)
Delay between reference and test (0.4 s vs 4 s)
Chromatic noise added to the reference stimulus
key_findings: Hue estimates were biased toward the mean of the current stimulus set
Bias magnitude grew with longer delay (more internal noise)
Bias magnitude grew with added chromatic noise (more external noise)
interpretations: Color estimation behaves like Bayesian inference combining a set-mean prior with a noise-scaled likelihood
